title: Establishment Clause note
summary: Government actions relating to religion may raise constitutional issues if they advance, favor, or entangle government with religion in unlawful ways.

title: Fifth Amendment / Due Process
summary: Protects due process against federal government action.

title: First Amendment
summary: Protects speech, press, religion, assembly, and petition.

title: Fourteenth Amendment
summary: Includes the Citizenship Clause and equal protection / due process guarantees.

title: Immigration due process note
summary: Administrative and constitutional protections can apply in immigration adjudication and status determinations.

title: War Crimes Act note
summary: This note is for **very high-threshold** allegations only. Public reporting alone is not enough to conclude criminal war-crimes liability.

title: War powers framework
summary: The Constitution and federal war-powers framework allocate responsibilities between Congress and the President concerning the use of force.
